basics.name: Yulin (Frank) Zhang
basics.label: Computer Science and Mathematics Undergraduate
basics.summary: Duke undergraduate studying Computer Science and Mathematics, with research and engineering experience in machine learning, NLP, interpretability, AI safety, and secure software systems.

work.summary: Built computer-vision systems for industrial safety analytics.
highlights: Implemented real-time multi-object detection pipelines in PyTorch and PaddleDetection using YOLOv11 to identify safety-critical items and human behaviors in industrial video streams.
Integrated ByteTrack, BotSort with ReID, and related methods to track objects through occlusions, detect wrong-way movement, and deliver automated safety alerts and behavior analytics.

work.summary: Trains and evaluates generative AI systems through RLHF, code reasoning, localization, and safety-alignment workflows.
highlights: Trained LLMs via RLHF to reduce hallucinations, improve localization, and align multilingual AI responses with prompt intent across culture, math, and coding.
Wrote efficient programs that outperform LLM code responses in code generation, reasoning, and refactoring.
Rated and edited generated text to improve response accuracy and safety alignment.

work.summary: Evaluates AI search agents under lazy-search and search-time contamination settings.
highlights: Evaluated agents such as Gemini Deep Research by rewriting benchmark questions and measuring robustness against injected distracting rewritten question-answer pairs.

work.summary: Works on interpretable and efficient decision-tree methods.
highlights: Designed algorithms for multiway-split decision trees using LightGBM preprocessing and an improved SPLIT framework to reduce depth, optimize decision sparsity, and improve expressivity, accuracy, and performance.

work.summary: Studies internal concept representations in LLMs.
highlights: Studying how LLMs form callable internal representations of concepts to provide a mechanistic interpretation of grokking and compression.

work.company: Michigan Institute for Data and AI in Society
work.summary: Researched Android app security, malware detection, and app-longevity prediction.
highlights: Investigated secure Android app use with VirusTotal, Quark-Engine, and custom ML classifiers built on XGBoost, ExcelFormer, and Trompt.
Trained an XGBoost model to predict whether an app would be removed from a web store, achieving 80%+ accuracy.
Created and validated extensive datasets with VirusTotal and Quark-Engine scan analysis results.

work.company: Living Systems Research Lab, University of Michigan
work.summary: Modeled dynamic cellular movement and biological pattern formation.
highlights: Animated biological phenomena such as morphogenesis, tissue growth, and pattern formation in MATLAB and Python.
Simulated complex biophysical processes based on tissue forces and tensions.

work.summary: Modeled car-crash casualty estimates for medical resource allocation.
highlights: Trained robust polynomial regression models in R to predict mean casualties from factors such as driver's age and lighting conditions, minimizing mean bias to 0.12 people per accident.

work.company: SoftCom Lab, Cal State Polytechnic University
work.summary: Built NLP-powered software for reducing digital distractions.
highlights: Developed Concentration, a Chrome extension using NLP, LLMs, Flask, and Firebase that reached 2.2K+ users and a 4.6/5 Chrome Web Store rating.
Filed provisional patent application 63/383,080 for the website-blocking algorithm.
Published and presented the related paper as lead author at the 9th International Conference on Software Engineering in Copenhagen, Denmark.

education.area: B.S. in Computer Science and Mathematics
B.S. in Computer Science and Mathematics, Honors Program

publications.name: Lazy Grounding: When Search Agents Misapply True Evidence
publications.summary: Zhang, Y.; Huang, Y.; Chen, S.; Lin, T.; Yang, Z.; Yin, X.; Dhingra, B.

publications.name: Interpretable Multiway-Split Trees
publications.summary: Chi, B.*; Zhang, Y.*; Zhong, C.; Rudin, C. *Equal contribution.

publications.name: An NLP-Learning Powered Customizable Approach Towards Auto-Blocking Distracting Websites
Rebuilding a Balanced Dataset of Removed and Non-Removed Android Apps Using VirusTotal and Quark Features
publications.summary: Mohsen, Fadi; Zhang, Y.; Bisgin, H.; Buchta, K.

publications.name: Beyond XGBoost: Predicting App Longevity in Google Play with ExcelFormer and Trompt
publications.summary: Bisgin, H.; Buchta, K.; Zhang, Y.; Mohsen, Fadi.

portfolio.name: MHacks 2024: MentalAI
portfolio.description: Led a team of 4 to fine-tune GPT-4o mini for mental health support; built the frontend with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, MongoDB, and the OpenAI API.

portfolio.name: Email Automation Service Development
portfolio.description: Built an open-source timed email delivery service with 25K+ downloads, attachment and embedded-image support, custom fonts, and 30% lower latency through persistent SMTP connections.

portfolio.name: Web Crawler Development for Uni Good News
portfolio.description: Built a multithreaded crawler that downloaded 1K+ news articles and checked daily for new content to support offline access for Uni Good News visitors.

portfolio.name: Concentration Chrome Extension
portfolio.description: Developed an NLP-powered Chrome extension with a Flask backend and Firebase database to auto-block distracting websites; reached 2.2K+ users and a 4.6/5 Chrome Web Store rating.
